title: StressResult
description: The output of running one of the six falsification tests against a fixture. If the test breaks the theory, the break is preserved here and must not be patched with prose.

test_name.description: Which of the six tests produced this result.

fixture_id.description: ID of the fixture being tested.

verdict.description: Outcome. theory_breaks = the test falsified or significantly pressured the claim for this fixture. Must be preserved. Do not patch with prose.

severity.description: How much pressure this result places on the theory. critical = theory cannot survive this case without revision.

what_was_tested.description: Exactly what claim or component of the theory was stress-tested in this run.

result_detail.description: What happened during the test. Precise, structural description. Not narrative defense of the theory.

revision_pressure.description: What the theory must clarify or revise based on this result.

breaks_preserved.description: Specific breaks, tensions, or contradictions this test produced. Each is a structural statement, not a rhetorical hedge.

surviving_claims.description: Claims that survived this test for this fixture.

demoted_claims.description: Claims that were demoted or weakened by this test.

next_test_recommended.description: What follow-up test or fixture would best resolve the remaining unresolved tension.

run_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when this stress test was run.
